agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being scoped for customer-facing delivery visibility. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements regarding whether courier GPS location should be exposed to customers. This is a classic two-sided marketplace tension: customer transparency vs. courier privacy and retention.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Order Fulfillment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver/delivery person physically transporting the package from origin to customer address"},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"GPS coordinates of the courier's device, updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery"},{"term":"Delivery","definition":"The fulfillment leg from dispatch to customer handoff, during which tracking visibility is relevant"},{"term":"Customer","definition":"The end recipient of a package who wants visibility into delivery progress"},{"term":"Tracking visibility","definition":"The degree to which a customer can observe courier progress — ranges from no visibility to exact GPS coordinates"}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"During an active delivery, the customer must receive some form of progress indication about their order","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy decision about customer experience, not a data integrity constraint. The level and form of visibility is a product decision.","testable_assertion":"When a delivery is in-progress and the customer views tracking, some progress information is displayed (not a blank screen)"},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Courier personally-identifiable location data must not be exposed in a way that creates safety or privacy risks for the courier","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Courier safety is a physical constraint — exposing exact coordinates creates real-world harm (stalking, confrontation, surveillance). A system that endangers its workforce is fundamentally corrupt regardless of customer preference.","testable_assertion":"No API response or UI element exposes raw GPS latitude/longitude of a courier to a customer"},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Courier location data collection and sharing must comply with applicable labor and privacy regulations","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory compliance is a hard constraint — violations expose the company to legal liability regardless of business preferences.","testable_assertion":"Location data sharing policies are documented and auditable, and courier consent is recorded before any location sharing occurs"},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The tracking experience must provide enough information for customers to plan for delivery arrival","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a UX quality bar, not a system integrity constraint. The threshold of 'enough' is a product decision.","testable_assertion":"Given an in-progress delivery, the customer can determine an estimated arrival window"}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A delivery is in-progress and assigned to a courier","when":"The customer views the tracking page","then":"The customer sees delivery progress information (status, ETA, or abstracted proximity) but NOT raw GPS coordinates"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A courier is actively delivering an order","when":"The system processes the courier's GPS coordinates","then":"Raw coordinates are stored server-side for operational use but are never transmitted to customer-facing clients"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A delivery has been completed or cancelled","when":"The customer views the order details","then":"No courier location data of any kind is displayed"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A courier has not yet consented to location-based features","when":"The system attempts to share any form of location-derived data with customers","then":"No location-derived information is shared for that courier's deliveries"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier GPS signal is lost mid-delivery","expected_behavior":"Customer sees last-known status with a stale-data indicator; system does not fabricate or interpolate location"},{"scenario":"Customer calls support demanding exact courier location","expected_behavior":"Support agents may see courier location for operational purposes but must not relay exact coordinates to the customer"},{"scenario":"Delivery is delayed and courier is stationary for extended period","expected_behavior":"System shows delivery status as delayed with updated ETA; does not reveal that courier is stationary at a specific location"},{"scenario":"Customer and courier are in the same building (last-mile)","expected_behavior":"System may show 'Courier is nearby' or 'Arriving now' but does not pinpoint floor/unit location of courier"},{"scenario":"Courier delivers multiple orders on a single route","expected_behavior":"Each customer sees only their own delivery progress; no information about other stops or route is leaked"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Courier retention directly impacts delivery capacity and recruitment costs (typically $3K-5K per driver). Losing drivers due to surveillance concerns is a concrete, measurable cost. Customer churn from poor tracking is real but can be mitigated with abstracted tracking (as proven by Uber, DoorDash, etc.).","operational":"Exact GPS sharing increases support volume (customers calling about courier behavior), creates liability exposure, and requires more complex data infrastructure. Abstracted tracking (zone-based, ETA-based) is operationally simpler and still satisfies customer needs.","user":"Customers want predictability, not surveillance — research consistently shows ETA accuracy matters more than map pin precision. Couriers want dignity and safety while working.","risk":"Exposing exact GPS creates legal risk (labor law, privacy regulations like GDPR/CCPA), physical safety risk to couriers, and reputational risk if a courier is harmed due to location exposure. NOT providing any tracking creates competitive disadvantage.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Will receive delivery progress visibility but not raw GPS — 90%+ of the transparency value with none of the liability"},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Protected from customer surveillance; location collected for ops but not exposed — directly addresses retention concern"},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Must reframe 'transparency' value proposition around delivery predictability rather than GPS voyeurism"},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Courier retention concern addressed; ops retains full GPS access for routing, dispatch, and performance management"},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Reduced exposure from not sharing PII-grade location data with external parties"}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct, explicit contradiction between two stakeholder teams, both presenting their positions as non-negotiable. No product owner or executive has arbitrated. The conflict MUST be resolved by someone with authority over both teams before implementation can proceed. Confidence is low because the actual business decision has not been made — only the options are clear.","high_confidence_areas":["The two requirements are genuinely contradictory and cannot both be satisfied as stated","Courier safety and privacy is a real constraint with legal and human implications","Abstracted tracking (ETA, zones, status) is a well-proven industry pattern that partially satisfies both sides","Raw GPS exposure to customers is not an industry norm and creates measurable risk"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which team has ultimate decision authority over this feature","Whether there are contractual or regulatory constraints already in place regarding courier location","Company's actual courier churn data and whether location sharing is the primary cause","Customer research on whether exact GPS vs. ETA-based tracking affects satisfaction or conversion"],"assumptions":["No existing regulatory or contractual obligation mandates either approach","The company operates a two-sided marketplace where both courier retention and customer satisfaction are business-critical","Industry-standard abstracted tracking would be an acceptable middle ground if both teams agree to compromise"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates visible to customers at all times. Operations requires courier location to never be shown to customers. These are explicit, mutually exclusive requirements from two teams, both stated as non-negotiable. A product or executive decision-maker must arbitrate.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success team","Operations team","Couriers","Customers","Legal/Compliance"],"options":["A: Full GPS visibility (satisfies Customer Success, violates Operations, creates legal/safety risk)","B: No location visibility at all (satisfies Operations, violates Customer Success, competitive disadvantage)","C: Abstracted tracking — ETA, delivery zones, status updates — without raw GPS (industry-standard compromise that partially satisfies both)","D: Tiered approach — couriers opt in to varying levels of visibility with informed consent"],"recommendation":"Option C (abstracted tracking) is the strong recommendation. It is the proven industry pattern (Uber, DoorDash, Amazon all use abstracted tracking, not raw GPS). It gives customers the predictability they actually want while protecting courier privacy. Customer Success can market 'real-time delivery tracking' without exposing coordinates. However, this decision MUST be made by someone with authority over both teams — Bird cannot resolve a stakeholder conflict, only identify the correct domain answer."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Exposing raw courier GPS coordinates to customers as proposed by Customer Success","business_rule_broken":"BR-2 — Courier location data must not be exposed in a way that creates safety or privacy risks. Exact GPS coordinates are PII-grade data that enable real-time surveillance of a worker."},{"violation":"Showing zero delivery progress information as implied by Operations' absolute position","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 — Customers must receive some form of progress indication. Complete opacity is a competitive disadvantage and fails basic customer experience standards in modern logistics."}]}

NOT providing any tracking creates competitive disadvantage.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Will receive delivery progress visibility but not raw GPS — 90%+ of the transparency value with none of the liability"},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Protected from customer surveillance; location collected for ops but not exposed — directly addresses retention concern"},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Must reframe 'transparency' value proposition around delivery predictability rather than GPS voyeurism"},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Courier retention concern addressed; ops retains full GPS access for routing, dispatch, and performance management"},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Reduced exposure from not sharing PII-grade location data with external parties"}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct, explicit contradiction between two stakeholder teams, both presenting their positions as non-negotiable. No product owner or executive has arbitrated. The conflict MUST be resolved by someone with authority over both teams before implementation can proceed. Confidence is low because the actual business decision has not been made — only the options are clear.","high_confidence_areas":["The two requirements are genuinely contradictory and cannot both be satisfied as stated","Courier safety and privacy is a real constraint with legal and human implications","Abstracted tracking (ETA, zones, status) is a well-proven industry pattern that partially satisfies both sides","Raw GPS exposure to customers is not an industry norm and creates measurable risk"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which team has ultimate decision authority over this feature","Whether there are contractual or regulatory constraints already in place regarding courier location","Company's actual courier churn data and whether location sharing is the primary cause","Customer research on whether exact GPS vs. ETA-based tracking affects satisfaction or conversion"],"assumptions":["No existing regulatory or contractual obligation mandates either approach","The company operates a two-sided marketplace where both courier retention and customer satisfaction are business-critical","Industry-standard abstracted tracking would be an acceptable middle ground if both teams agree to compromise"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates visible to customers at all times. A product or executive decision-maker must arbitrate.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success team","Operations team","Couriers","Customers","Legal/Compliance"],"options":["A: Full GPS visibility (satisfies Customer Success, violates Operations, creates legal/safety risk)","B: No location visibility at all (satisfies Operations, violates Customer Success, competitive disadvantage)","C: Abstracted tracking — ETA, delivery zones, status updates — without raw GPS (industry-standard compromise that partially satisfies both)","D: Tiered approach — couriers opt in to varying levels of visibility with informed consent"],"recommendation":"Option C (abstracted tracking) is the strong recommendation. It is the proven industry pattern (Uber, DoorDash, Amazon all use abstracted tracking, not raw GPS). It gives customers the predictability they actually want while protecting courier privacy. Customer Success can market 'real-time delivery tracking' without exposing coordinates. However, this decision MUST be made by someone with authority over both teams — Bird cannot resolve a stakeholder conflict, only identify the correct domain answer."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Exposing raw courier GPS coordinates to customers as proposed by Customer Success","business_rule_broken":"BR-2 — Courier location data must not be exposed in a way that creates safety or privacy risks. Exact GPS coordinates are PII-grade data that enable real-time surveillance of a worker."},{"violation":"Showing zero delivery progress information as implied by Operations' absolute position","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 — Customers must receive some form of progress indication. Complete opacity is a competitive disadvantage and fails basic customer experience standards in modern logistics."}]}
